title: Form Activity Report
subtitle: 28 live forms across roller.software
intro: This report maps every lead-capture form on the marketing site to the page(s) it lives on and how recently it last received a real submission. Recency is a reliable signal of whether a form is pulling its weight. Use it to decide which forms to keep, which to reposition, and which to retire, and to focus a traffic conversation on the pages that matter.
callout: The Last submission and status columns are measured from live HubSpot data. The Volume column is intentionally blank — total submission counts are not in this pull and need a HubSpot export.

legend.label: Buried — high value but low placement
Dormant / event — retire or archive candidate

readingGuide.heading: Active (top of the table)
readingGuide.body: These forms convert on their own. Protect the pages that feed them and keep them on freshness monitoring.

readingGuide.heading: Buried, high value (Demo, Enterprise, FEC)
readingGuide.body: These are the most important finding. The forms work and the topics are commercial, but submissions are rare because the form sits low on a low-traffic page. These are redesign and reposition candidates, not retire candidates. Moving the form up, tightening the offer, or driving more traffic should lift them.

readingGuide.heading: Slow (weeks between submissions)
readingGuide.body: Still alive but underperforming. Decide case by case whether the topic justifies a refresh or better placement.

readingGuide.heading: Dormant (months to years) and event forms
readingGuide.body: These have effectively stopped. Gated content silent for a year or more (GX Show, Guest Experience Playbook, Customer Referral) is the clearest retire list. Event forms are expected to be dead between events and can be archived. Confirm seasonality on the trends and school-vacation guides before retiring, since those may spike at a particular time of year.

method: Forms were identified from the June 2026 site audit and matched to their HubSpot form ID by reading each live page. Recency is the time since each form's most recent HubSpot submission at the time of this report. Forms sharing one HubSpot form ID across several pages are listed once, with all their pages. The site-wide subscribe form is listed separately because it appears on nearly every page.
footer: Generated 01 July 2026. Recency reflects a point-in-time HubSpot pull and will drift; re-run the freshness checks for current values. Volume column pending a HubSpot submission-count export.

forms.name: AIP Awards 2025

forms.note: The only recent submission is a manual test; no organic traffic.

forms.name: Demo request
forms.recommendation: Reposition. High-value intent, but form is buried. Move it up / redesign, do not retire.
forms.note: One demo form shared across three high-intent pages; sits low on the page.

forms.name: Enterprise
forms.recommendation: Reposition. High-value, low placement. Redesign the page around the form.
forms.note: Form sits low on the page.

forms.recommendation: Retire candidate. Note possible seasonality before deciding.

forms.recommendation: Keep. Core lead path, monitored on freshness.

forms.name: Site-wide subscribe
pages: Almost every page (also the only form on /blog and /customer-stories)
forms.recommendation: Keep. Global form, monitored on freshness.
